topic.what: Emergency alert spoofing attack in 5G networks using rogue base stations and cross-cell verification as a lightweight defense
topic.how: Modified OpenAirInterface RAN to broadcast forged SIB8 messages via SDR; UE compares alerts across neighboring cells to detect single-source spoofing
topic.why_matters: Public warning systems lack authentication, enabling attackers to trigger fake emergencies causing panic or phishing; no prior open-source 5G implementation existed

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of devices displaying forged alerts when camped on rogue gNB
characteristics.evidence: All 5 tested devices (Fairphone 5, Samsung Tab S10 Ultra, Nothing Phone 3a, Samsung A36 5G, iPhone 14 Pro) displayed alerts immediately upon camping
characteristics.context: Tested in controlled lab environment with USRP B210 SDR

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether attack requires legitimate core network infrastructure
characteristics.evidence: Attack succeeded running only modified gNB without AMF or core network; alerts displayed before RACH initiation
characteristics.context: Attack can be executed with just commodity laptop and SDR

characteristics.evidence: Fairphone 5 (Android/e/OS), Samsung Tab S10 Ultra (Android 16), Nothing Phone 3a (Android 16), Samsung A36 5G (Android 16), iPhone 14 Pro (iOS 26.1)
characteristics.context: Spans Android and iOS platforms with recent OS versions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Device capability to handle multi-segment warning messages
characteristics.evidence: Samsung tablet failed to display multi-segment alerts; messages exceeding 15 pages not displayed on any device
